title: Syracuse city budget under Mayor Walsh increased by $102 million over eight-year term
summary: Mayor Ben Walsh's proposed FY 2025-2026 budget of $348 million represents a $102 million increase over his eight-year tenure. The Common Council has filed 80 amendments and proposed cuts, with the Mayor committing to review each before announcing vetoes. City Auditor Alex Marion attributes budget growth primarily to labor contracts and salary increases, with notable funding increases in police ($48M to $65M), fire ($33M to $48M), and DPW ($32M to $40M) departments.

political_signal.description: Common Council challenging Mayor's $348M budget proposal with 80 amendments and proposed cuts; independent audit previously identified 26 red flags; labor cost drivers and public safety spending increases under investigation.
